title: Language model-guided anticipation and discovery of mammalian metabolites
core_conclusion: DeepMet, a chemical language model trained on 2,046 known human metabolites, anticipates previously unrecognized mammalian metabolites by learning SMILES string grammar from metabolite-like chemical space. Integrated with CFM-ID MS/MS prediction and a meta-learning classifier, the pipeline achieves 70% annotation accuracy in 23 mouse tissues and substantially expands spectral annotation of 29.1 million MS/MS spectra from 4,510 human blood analyses, revealing several dozen new structurally diverse mammalian metabolites including potential clinical biomarkers.
why_it_matters: Illuminates metabolic dark matter by systematically generating candidate structures for unidentified mass spectrometry peaks; prospectively predicted 81% of 313 new HMDB v5.0 metabolites; expands annotatable human blood metabolome; demonstrates clinical biomarker potential (sepsis); ships open web app and Snakemake pipeline.
why_nature: Paradigm shift from rule-based to language-model metabolite discovery; broad cross-disciplinary impact; large-scale validation (1 billion SMILES, 29.1 million spectra, 17 confirmed new metabolites); platform contribution (deepmet.org + GitHub pipeline).
added_value_to_my_research: Sampling-frequency prior -> spatial plausibility scoring for GeoAI feature imputation in data-sparse regions
Meta-learning calibration of heterogeneous signals -> multi-source spatial reliability calibrator for health GeoAI
End-to-end generation + scoring -> physics-informed graph learning for joint spatial prediction and uncertainty
Tissue-resolved heterogeneity -> MGWR/GWR application to spatially varying omics signals in biomedical data
Re-interrogation of public metabolomic repositories -> FAIR gap-filling framework for under-surveyed geospatial health data
